content.full_text:
CITY OF La Cañada Flintridge

Mayor
GEORGE L PARRISH

City Council
O. WARREN HILLGREN
EDMUND J KRAUSE
MICHAEL C. MOUNT
J. D. SMITH

February 28, 1978


Mr. Ted Prim
Chief, Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Dear Mr. Prim:

This is in response to your letter of February 23, concerning
my earlier letter of February 7 concerning regulation 18116.
Your point is well taken that it is probably unlikely that
there will be local holidays which will not be State holidays
which are likely to be filing dates. I am not aware of all of
the dates which are likely to be filing dates, especially for
charter cities which have different election dates, but some
of the following holidays might be different for cities than
for the states: Lincoln's Birthday, Armed Forces Day, Columbus
Day, Veteran's Day, and the day after Thanksgiving. It is
probably unlikely that cities would have very many special
holidays exclusive to that city, but it would seem to me that
as long as a regulation was being adopted, it should probably
take into account the possibility of that sort of conflict.

I thank you for your response and appreciate your consideration
of this matter.

Very truly yours,

Clark L. Smithson
City Manager

CLS:nm


Post Office Box 456  •  La Cañada Flintridge, California 91011  •  (213) 790-8880 · 681-3266

Mayor
GEORGE L PARRISH

City Council
O WARREN HILLGREN
EDMUND J KRAUSE
MICHAEL C MOUNT
J D SMITH

CITY OF La Cañada Flintridge

February 7, 1978

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California
95804

Gentlemen:        ORDER ADOPTING REGULATIONS OF THE
                  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION,
                  SECTION 181.16

I have received your notice concerning adoption on December 7, 1977
of a regulation specifying that whenever a statement or report is
to be filed by a specific date and that date falls on a Saturday,
Sunday or official State holiday, the filing date for such statement or report shall be extended to the next regular business day.
I would hope that the regulation would be further modified to
provide that in the event statements or reports are to be filed
with a city or county government, and the deadline falls on a
holiday recognized by the city or county, the filing deadline shall
be extended to the next regular business day.

In many cases, cities have a different set of holidays than does
the State, so that the regulation adopted by the FPPC would not
be helpful in many cases. For instance, although the State only
recognizes Thanksgiving Day, many cities give the day after
Thanksgiving also as a holiday. According to your regulations,
the filing deadline would now be the day after Thanksgiving.
However, with the city offices being closed in some cities on
the day after Thanksgiving, the effective date of filing would
still have to be the day before Thanksgiving.

Thank you very much for your consideration.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Clark L. Smithson
                                        City Manager

sections.question_synthetic: The city manager is responding to FPPC feedback regarding regulation 18116 and the potential for local holidays that differ from state holidays to conflict with filing dates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence from a city manager responding to FPPC feedback, not an FPPC advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. References a prior letter of February 7 and FPPC's response of February 23. Document discusses regulation 18116 and holiday/filing date conflicts.

embedding.summary: This is a letter from City Manager Clark L. Smithson of La Canada Flintridge responding to FPPC's February 23 correspondence about regulation 18116 and the conflict between local and state holidays as filing dates. The city manager acknowledges FPPC's point about the unlikelihood of local holidays creating conflicts but suggests the regulation should account for the possibility.
